quizzes.question: What is the term for the party who initiates a proposal in a contract?
What is required for a contract to be valid according to the 'meeting of the minds' principle?
Why was Sunil denied the reward in the case study provided?
options: He failed to catch the convict
He had no knowledge of the offer
quizzes.answer: He had no knowledge of the offer

quizzes.question: Which of the following is considered a ground for a contract to be void as against public policy?
In the BR Industries scenario, why did the court uphold the contract despite the profit margin dispute?
options: Business entities at arms-length are governed by agreed consideration
The defendant failed to show up
The contract was written by the government
quizzes.answer: Business entities at arms-length are governed by agreed consideration

quizzes.question: Can a contract be voided simply because it resulted in a very high profit for one party?
options: Yes, if the court deems it greedy
No, provided there is no fraud and parties are at arms-length
Only if it involves a public official
quizzes.answer: No, provided there is no fraud and parties are at arms-length

quizzes.question: What is the primary requirement for accepting a 'General Offer' such as a reward?
options: Witnesses present at the time of act
Knowledge of the offer at the time of performance
quizzes.answer: Knowledge of the offer at the time of performance

quizzes.question: Which of these is NOT listed as a form of void contract under public policy?
What does 'Consensus ad Idem' literally signify in a contract?
How do courts typically treat 'bad deals' between business entities?
options: They rewrite them to be fair
They treat them as void by default
